name: South Holland (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the gold field of the historic arms of Holland

description: A yellow field charged with a red rampant lion, armed and langued, in the centre.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 22 February 1985. The flag is a banner of the historic arms of the County of Holland (or, a lion rampant gules), in use since the 13th century, and replaced an earlier purely-yellow provincial flag.

features.symbolism: the yellow field reproduces the gold (or) field of the historic arms of the County of Holland
the rampant red lion of the historic County of Holland, attested in the arms since the 13th century
